title: Fund Administration Manager
description:
We are currently seeking a Fund Administration Senior Manager/Manager who will oversee the day-to-day operations of fund accounting, administration, and reporting for investment funds (e.g., traditional funds, exchange traded funds, hedge funds, private equity funds). This role ensures accurate NAV calculations, report deliverables, compliance with regulatory requirements, and efficient coordination between fund managers, investors, auditors, and other service providers (e.g., tax advisors, corporate secretary, etc).

Responsibilities

• Oversee the calculation of Net Asset Value (NAV) for funds (e.g., traditional funds, exchange traded funds, hedge funds, private equity funds) and ensure accuracy of fund accounting records which includes reconciliation and processing of trades, corporate actions, accruals and expense allocations, and the calculation of daily, monthly, quarterly Net Asset Values (NAV), valuation methodologies and KPI of the team.
• Prepare, review and approve financial statements, investor reports, etc.
• Ensure the financials are in compliance with fund governing documents (Private Placement Memorandum, Supplements, LPA, etc) and regulatory requirements.
• Handle fund investors’ queries. Review capital call and distribution notices.
• Knowledge/experience in Transfer Agency (TA) roles and responsibilities is a must. Demonstrate experience overseeing TA operations, with strong understanding of the end-to-end TA lifecycle, investor register maintenance, and transaction processing controls.
• Co-ordinate with auditors for annual audits and ensure timely regulatory filings.
• Manage and co-ordinate client funds’ compliance and regulatory requirements involving auditors, corporate secretary, tax advisors and legal counsels.
• Drive Fund Administration and Transfer Agency system rollout initiatives by coordinating with external vendors, IT and other stakeholders to ensure smooth operational transitions for new fund services initiatives.
• Acts as a key contact point for fund managers and investors regarding fund related enquiries and facilitate clients’ due diligence visits. Establish service level agreements with clients.
• Handle adhoc duties and group –wide projects, including new fund or entity set-ups, to support the operational and fund accounting needs of the business and management.

Requirements:

• A Bachelor’s degree in Accountancy or related field, and preferably be a qualified Singapore Chartered Accountant (CA) or other equivalent qualifications.
• At least 10 years of working experience and 5 years of relevant experience in fund administration, and knowledge of preparing funds’ financial statements is a must.
• Minimum 3 years of leading a team will be an added advantage. To lead, guide and motivate team members.
• Potential to act as Team leader to manage the allocation of work to respective team members, ensure completeness of work deliverables and ensure team achieve objective of the management.
• Good working knowledge of investment products, investment operations and familiarity with fund regulations and accounting standards. Familiarity with various asset classes, such as equities, fixed income, derivatives, and alternative investments. Knowledge to draft Financial statements in accordance to US GAAP and IFRS standards for Investment Funds.
• Strong analytical skills and detail-oriented.
• Strong leadership skills with good written, verbal communication skills in English and Mandarin so as to manage regular communication with Chinese speaking customers and manage Chinese business reports to Head Office.
• Experience in fund accounting software is a must, coupled with advanced proficiency in Microsoft Office, particularly Excel.

districts.location: D01 Cecil, Marina, People’s Park, Raffles Place
